title: Use claude -p with --append-system-prompt for agent personas
source: Claude Code CLI --append-system-prompt flag for injecting system-level instructions
description: Agent personas and system instructions are currently embedded in the user prompt (build_prompt in delegate.py), mixing instructions with task content. Claude Code supports --append-system-prompt to inject system-level instructions separately, which Claude treats with higher authority than user-prompt instructions. This provides better prompt injection resistance and cleaner separation of persona from task.
proposed_changes: Split build_prompt() in delegate.py into two parts: (1) a system prompt containing the agent persona, role instructions, security boundaries, and SANDWICH_REINFORCEMENT, and (2) a user prompt containing only the task description, ticket context, and project structure. Pass the system prompt via `--append-system-prompt` flag in the claude subprocess call. This cleanly separates trusted instructions from potentially untrusted task content, improving the effectiveness of the existing prompt injection defenses in security_utils.py.
